# Pre-Plan Requirements Quality Checklist: SpecOps CLI

**Purpose**: Formal pre-plan gate — validates completeness, clarity, and consistency of the requirements in spec.md before `/speckit-plan` runs. Focus: initialization & additive injection, ledger & phases, review flow, plus critical NFRs (Speckit compatibility, offline behavior).
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Initialization & Additive Injection

- [ ] CHK001 Is "the Speckit structure" that initialization must detect defined with concrete, verifiable criteria (which files/directories constitute presence)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the "implementation prompt" and "planning prompt" identified precisely enough to locate the target files across real Speckit installations (names, paths, or a discovery rule)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the directive-block marker format specified, or explicitly delegated to the plan phase? [Gap, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for a partially valid Speckit installation (expected prompt file missing, renamed, or relocated by a Speckit upgrade)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-003/§FR-006]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the idempotency requirement cover corrupted or half-present markers (begin without end, duplicated begin), not just the clean re-run case? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the byte-identical restore guarantee consistent with in-place block updates after a Speckit upgrade changes the underlying file? [Consistency, Spec §SC-010/§FR-007]
- [ ] CHK007 Are requirements defined for re-running initialization when `specops.json` already exists with user-customized values? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the non-interactive initialization contract complete for CI use — flag name semantics, default decline behavior, exit code, and message? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK009 Does "assets in final form" define whether placeholder substitution (e.g., feature name in the ledger scaffold) is permitted at install time, or must files be copied verbatim? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK010 Is uninstallation (reversing `specops init`) explicitly in scope or explicitly excluded? [Gap, Scope]

## Ledger & Phases

- [ ] CHK011 Is the ledger's field-level schema (statuses, recovery block, phases, evidence entries) specified in requirements or explicitly delegated to the vendored reference (`status-template.yaml`)? [Completeness, Key Entities]
- [x] CHK012 Are the task-identifier matching rules between Speckit's `tasks.md` format (e.g., `T001` checklist lines) and the ledger sync defined? The reference implementation parses `### task-XX` headings, which Speckit does not produce. [Conflict, Spec §FR-008a] — **Resolved 2026-07-05**: FR-008a now defines identifiers as Speckit's own (`T001`), extracted from task checklist lines and stored verbatim; checkbox state not consumed; transition line generalized in FR-006, Assumptions, constitution v1.1.2, and lifecycle-roles.md.
- [ ] CHK013 Are requirements defined for tasks that are removed or renamed in `tasks.md` after having been registered (or completed) in the ledger? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK014 Is behavior specified when `start-task` runs while another task is already `IN_PROGRESS` (allowed? blocked? recovery point overwritten?)? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [x] CHK015 Does the ordered-progression rule of the phase machine account for the corrective loop (REVIEW back to IMPLEMENT on rejection) described in the lifecycle map, or does it forbid it as an out-of-order jump? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008b] — **Resolved 2026-07-05**: FR-008b now defines `REVIEW → IMPLEMENT` with result `REJECTED` as the single valid exception, each corrective round registering a new review cycle; lifecycle-roles.md aligned.
- [ ] CHK016 Are the allowed `result` values of a phase transition (e.g., APPROVED, REJECTED) specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008b]
- [ ] CHK017 Is deferring the evidence `<CLASS>` vocabulary to the reference scripts acceptable for planning, or should the classes (CLI_LOG, TEST_REPORT, SCREENSHOT_PATH, CODE_DIFF) be enumerated as requirements? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK018 Are automatic-evidence requirements defined for a task spanning multiple commits (the reference implementation harvests HEAD only)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK019 Is behavior specified when the test command succeeds but its output is unusable for the TEST_REPORT evidence summary? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK020 Are ledger-corruption scenarios (invalid YAML, hand-edited file) addressed with defined command behavior? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Review Flow

- [ ] CHK021 Is "the plan's declared scope" defined precisely enough for metadata-only rejection (which plan section enumerates the authorized paths)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for a missing or unresolvable required skill, and for an empty `skills_dir`? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK023 Is the location and numbering rule of `revisions/revision-X.md` specified relative to the spec workspace? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK024 Are the review decision outcomes (APPROVED/REJECTED) and their effects on the ledger phase machine specified as requirements? [Gap, Spec §FR-013/§FR-008b]
- [ ] CHK025 Does FR-013's mandatory order include the lint/test mechanical pre-filter that the reference review prompt requires, and is `lint_command`'s role consistent between FR-004 and FR-013? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004/§FR-013]
- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements defined for a review invoked with an empty diff (no changes since baseline)? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Gates & Exit Codes

- [ ] CHK027 Are exit-code semantics distinguished between a blocking validation failure and an unexpected execution error (crash, unreadable file), or intentionally identical? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the reference behavior of exempting human-marked commits (`(human)`) from reconciliation in scope, out of scope, or unaddressed? [Assumption, Spec §FR-011]

## Critical Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK029 Are supported Speckit versions/layouts documented as a dependency or assumption (what happens on an unknown future layout)? [Dependency, Gap]
- [ ] CHK030 Is offline operation specified for all commands, or only for initialization (SC-009)? [Coverage, Spec §SC-009]
- [ ] CHK031 Can the English-only requirement be objectively verified for generated content vs client-authored configuration values (which are outside SpecOps control)? [Measurability, Spec §FR-014]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items flagged [Conflict] (CHK012, CHK015) are the highest-priority findings — both indicate the spec inherited assumptions from the reference implementation that do not hold in the Speckit context.
- Resolve or consciously defer all items before running `/speckit-plan`.
